children.name: Aegithalos
children.description: Tiny tail-heavy birds that move through woodland in chattering flocks. They build one of the most elaborate nests in European ornithology — an oval elastic cocoon of moss and lichen woven with spider silk and lined with hundreds of feathers, expandable as the chicks grow. In hard winters they huddle together for warmth at roost.

children.name: Aegithalos caudatus
children.description: A tiny, fluffy, long-tailed bird that travels in chattering family flocks through woodland and scrub. Northern Swedish birds have pure-white heads and build an extraordinary elastic domed nest of moss, lichen, and spider silk.

children.name: Aegithalos glaucogularis
children.description: A central and eastern Chinese long-tailed tit, formerly treated as a race of the Long-tailed Tit, with a delicate silvery throat patch.

children.name: Aegithalos concinnus
children.description: A colourful Himalayan and Southeast Asian bushtit with a chestnut cap, black mask and throat, moving in restless flocks through hill forest.

children.name: Aegithalos iouschistos
children.description: A buff-and-rufous bushtit of the central and eastern Himalayas, foraging in mossy high-altitude fir and rhododendron forest.

children.name: Aegithalos bonvaloti
children.description: A bushtit of the mountains of southwestern China and adjacent Myanmar, distinguished by its broad black eyebrow and pale crown.

children.name: Aegithalos fuliginosus
children.description: A sooty-grey bushtit endemic to central China, with a pale eye-ring giving it a wide-eyed expression, inhabiting montane forests.

children.name: Aegithalos niveogularis
children.description: A high-altitude bushtit of the western Himalayas, ranging up to the treeline in birch and conifer forest with a snowy-white throat.

children.name: Aegithalos leucogenys
children.description: A bushtit of the mountains of Afghanistan and Pakistan, marked by clean white cheeks and a brown crown.

children.name: Aegithalos sharpei
children.description: A bushtit of the hills of Myanmar, sometimes treated as a race of the Black-throated Bushtit but distinguished by its plainer face pattern.

children.name: Psaltria
children.description: A monotypic genus for the pygmy bushtit, the smallest member of the family, endemic to the mountains of Java.

children.name: Psaltria exilis
children.description: A minuscule, drab bushtit of Java's montane forests, among the smallest of all passerines, moving in tight twittering flocks through the canopy.

children.name: Psaltriparus
children.description: A monotypic genus for the American bushtit, the only member of the family in the New World.

children.name: Psaltriparus minimus
children.description: A tiny grey bird of western North American scrub and oak woodland, weaving a hanging gourd-shaped nest of spider silk and travelling in busy flocks.

children.name: Leptopoecile
children.description: The tit-warblers, brilliantly coloured small birds of high Central Asian mountains, washed in violet, blue, and lilac.

children.name: Leptopoecile sophiae
children.description: A jewel-like bird of high alpine scrub in Central Asia and the Himalayas, with a lilac body, blue crown, and white brow, sought after by birdwatchers in winter.

children.name: Leptopoecile elegans
children.description: A short-tailed, pinkish-and-lilac tit-warbler of the high conifer forests of the Tibetan Plateau, topped with a pale silvery crest.
